title: ferray-random: binomial uses normal approximation for large n*p instead of BTPE algorithm
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

binomial_single() in discrete.rs uses a normal approximation with rejection for np >= 30: 'let z = standard_normal_single(bg); let x = (np + sigma * z + 0.5).floor()'. The comment says '(BTPE is complex; this is a simpler but adequate approach)'. NumPy uses the BTPE (Binomial, Triangle, Parallelogram, Exponential) algorithm from Hormann 1993, which is exact and efficient for all n*p. The normal approximation can produce incorrect distributional properties (skewness, kurtosis) for moderate n*p values (30-100). Implement BTPE for correctness.
